page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs based on the Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, with 24 physical cores and a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile characterized by average multi-core capabilities but weak single-core execution. The single-core performance is positioned in the bottom 25% for both stress-ng and Geekbench tests, with single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression falling into the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-threaded workloads perform in the middle 50%, as demonstrated by average scores in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth also remains average across both small and large block read/write operations.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.g5.6xlarge is its low single-threaded efficiency relative to its multi-threaded capacity. While single-core tasks and text processing are weak, the instance maintains average throughput for multi-threaded operations, database tasks, and static web serving. It is suitable for general-purpose workloads that can leverage parallel processing, such as multi-threaded software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations, but it is less efficient for single-threaded processing or memory-intensive tasks due to its lower memory benchmark scores.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 24 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a significant divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core execution and single-threaded compression tasks rank in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware. However, the instance achieves average, middle-tier results in multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations. With an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, the ecs.g5.6xlarge is suited for parallelizable, general-purpose workloads such as multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations, though it presents a clear tradeoff for single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96 GB RAM. It offers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core processing capabilities.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96 GB RAM, delivering average multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core instance offering balanced memory-to-CPU allocation for parallelizable general-purpose workloads with moderate network throughput.

KVM hypervisor with 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth
Weak single-core and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for multi-threaded compilation, web serving, and database operations
